item.title: A Wondrous Array of Boundary Pushers at SummerStage
item.description: Also: Lucy Sante’s poignant humor, American Ballet Theatre’s summer season, the incisive melodrama of Satyajit Ray, and more.

item.title: All That Glimmers at Ambassadors Clubhouse
item.description: Theatricality goes only so far at the ambitious new restaurant from the group behind London’s Gymkhana.
item.dc:creator: ByHelen Rosner

item.title: Why Todd Blanche Should Not Be Attorney General
item.description: In a Senate that took its constitutional role seriously, Blanche would not win confirmation a second time.
item.dc:creator: ByRuth Marcus

item.title: On the Front Lines of Delaney Hall,ICE’sNewark Prison
item.description: The detention center was the first new facility to open under the second Trump Administration. Protesters won’t stop until it is shut down.
item.dc:creator: ByIan Frazier

item.title: Melanie Hamrick Is Still On Pointe
item.description: Since retiring from the American Ballet Theatre, the dancer has expanded her pursuits—writing romance books and choreographing shows with the help of her fiancé, Mick Jagger.
item.dc:creator: ByBob Morris

item.title: Jackie Gleason’s Paranormal Activity
item.description: The “Honeymooners” actor was obsessed with the supernatural—even his house looked like a spacecraft. On a tour of the Mothership, will the author of a new Gleason book come face to face with the extraterrestrial?
item.dc:creator: ByBruce Handy

item.title: Beyond Banners
item.description: A few things to add to the M.S.G. rafters to commemorate the 2026 N.B.A. Finals.
item.dc:creator: ByBenjamin Schwartz

item.title: Misery Loves Company—If There Are Snacks
item.description: Do “Admin Nights” make people more productive or less lonely?
item.dc:creator: ByPatricia Marx

item.title: Jürgen Habermas Defended Reason in a Darkening Age
item.description: The great German philosopher, who died in March, understood how much depended on a principled public sphere.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Ross

item.title: Inside the Ludicrous, Deadly Serious Plan to Take Over Greenland
item.description: “We want Greenland,” Trump said. Four men sprang into action to make fantasy a reality.
item.dc:creator: ByBen Taub

item.title: Ken Griffin’s Billions and Billions
item.description: The hedge-fund titan is an unabashed big spender—from pièds-a-terre to politics.
item.dc:creator: ByGary Sernovitz

item.title: Meet Russ Freud
item.description: Under its new director, Bob’s Insane Asylum is seeking residents out rather than just sitting back and waiting for them.
item.dc:creator: ByIan Frazier

item.title: “Mutter”
item.description: I tell my mother that sometimes it feels like an emergency: she must know that no one else can ever be what she is for me.
item.dc:creator: ByEsther Yi

item.title: How Did American Christianity End Up Like This?
item.description: History helps explain the particular faith that now rules our religious marketplace.
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Luo

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “Ghost-Eye,” “Whistler,” “Newcomers,” and “Fires in the Night.”

item.title: When Did White-Collar Work Start to Look So Bleak?
item.description: In the nineteen-eighties, an office job promised security and fulfillment. For graduates starting careers today, the prospect is often tinged with dread.
item.dc:creator: ByMolly Fischer

item.title: Gustavo Dudamel and James Conlon Exit L.A.
item.description: Two progressive conductors depart, but Frank Gehry’s legacy to the city is growing.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Ross

item.title: The Hell-Raising Rocker Who Conquered Country Radio
item.description: Koe Wetzel, who broke out with a song about driving drunk to Taco Bell, reintroduces himself on a new album, “The Night Champion.”
item.dc:creator: ByKelefa Sanneh

item.title: In “Disclosure Day,” Steven Spielberg Replays the Hits
item.description: His new alien thriller has shades of “Close Encounters of the Third Kind,” “Minority Report,” even “The Post”—but the retreads yield diminishing returns.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “Sectioned”
item.description: “I wanted to sleep, / having been cut in half; / having been turned from one to two.”
item.dc:creator: ByMeghan O’Rourke

item.title: “American Pokeberry”
item.description: “Thrashers and cardinals dart / through the brush, through / the season.”
item.dc:creator: ByJanuary Gill O’Neil

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the June 22, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Tuesday, June 9, 2026
item.description: A moderately challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByWill Nediger
